_doc: Per-state licensing requirements for life / health / annuity / mortgage_protection. Every cell starts as research_pending=true. Populate via cited research batch (pattern: migration 0059b — source_url + source_quote on every value, omit rather than invent). DO NOT hand-edit invented values. Schema is frozen 2026-06-02 — bump _schema_version on shape changes.

_lines.label: Life
_lines.notes: Term, whole, IUL, UL, final expense

_lines.label: Health
_lines.notes: Med Supp, MAPD, ACA, dental, vision, group health

_lines.label: Annuity
_lines.notes: Some states fold annuity authority into Life — others require separate. Verify per state.

_lines.label: Mortgage Protection
_lines.notes: Sold under Life LoA; not a separate license — but check state-specific marketing-name rules

_cell_schema.research_pending: boolean — true until a cited row is filled in
_cell_schema.prelicense_hours_required: integer hours; null if state requires no formal pre-licensing course
_cell_schema.prelicense_required_course: boolean — does state mandate a state-approved pre-licensing course
_cell_schema.approved_course_vendors: array of { name, url, source_url } — state-approved pre-licensing providers
_cell_schema.exam_vendor: string enum: PSI | PEARSON_VUE | PROMETRIC | EXAMFX | OTHER
_cell_schema.fingerprint_code: string — the agency/ORI/service code applicants give the fingerprint vendor
_cell_schema.ce_hours_per_cycle: integer continuing-education hours per renewal cycle
_cell_schema.reciprocity_notes: string — does this state grant non-resident license freely?
_cell_schema.source_url: string — citable URL the values trace to (DOI, NIPR, statute)
_cell_schema.source_quote: string — verbatim quote from source_url proving the value

_step_by_step_template.title: Confirm eligibility
_step_by_step_template.what: Age 18+, SSN, US resident or work-authorized, no disqualifying criminal history (varies by state).

_step_by_step_template.title: Complete pre-licensing
_step_by_step_template.what: If state requires it, enroll in an approved course and complete required hours per line. Skip if state waives.

_step_by_step_template.title: Schedule + take state exam
_step_by_step_template.what: Register with the state's exam vendor (PSI / Pearson VUE / Prometric). Pay exam fee. Take exam; pass at state's threshold.

_step_by_step_template.title: Submit fingerprints
_step_by_step_template.what: If required, schedule via the state's fingerprint vendor using the state-issued service code. Pay processing fee.

_step_by_step_template.title: Apply for license
_step_by_step_template.what: Submit application via NIPR (nipr.com) or state DOI portal. Pay application fee. Upload exam result + fingerprint clearance.

_step_by_step_template.title: Get appointed
_step_by_step_template.what: License is dormant until at least one carrier appoints the producer. Use Repflow → Carriers tab to start carrier-appointment requests.

_step_by_step_template.title: First sale + CE planning
_step_by_step_template.what: Calendar renewal date and CE hours. Set Repflow reminders for the renewal cycle.

_research_batch_instructions: Populate via a parallel research-agent batch (mirror migration 0059b methodology). Each agent gets one (state, line) pair and the cell schema above. Agents MUST either fill every required field with source_url + source_quote — OR set research_pending=true with a brief reason. Inventing values = automatic reject. Cited sources should be NAIC, NIPR, the state's DOI producer page, or state statute. Avoid third-party blogs (Kaplan, ExamFX marketing pages, etc.) as primary sources — they're OK as secondary corroboration but not as source_url.

health.license_type_note: Alabama Life, Accident & Health license. 20-hour pre-licensing required. Exam via Pearson VUE.
health.source_quote: License Application Fee: $80. Exam Required for Life, Accident & Health or Sickness... Fingerprint checks are required for Insurance Producer applicants, specifically including Accident & Health or Sickness as a required line of authority.

mortgage_protection.license_type_note: Mortgage protection is sold under the Life LoA - no separate license required.
mortgage_protection.marketing_rule_notes: Ad must not use a policy name/title misrepresenting coverage's true nature (mortgage protection is term life); cannot imply affiliation with mortgage lender or government; cannot be untrue, deceptive, or misleading. Must identify carrier and product as life insurance; use approved form numbers; substantiate guaranteed-acceptance, no-exam, premium, and death-benefit claims; retain ad copies. State adopts NAIC Model 570 life-ad disclosure/recordkeeping rule.
mortgage_protection.marketing_rule_statute: Ala. Code §§ 27-12-1 to 27-12-23 [UTPA, NAIC Model 880]; Ala. Admin. Code r. 482-1-132 [NAIC Model 570]

life.source_quote: Alabama Life Producer Examination. 100 scored items, 150 minutes, 70% passing score.

content_outline.domain: I. Types of Policies
topics: Traditional Whole Life Products (Ordinary, Limited-pay, Single-premium)
Interest/Market-Sensitive Products (Universal life, Variable whole life, Variable universal life, Indexed life)
Term Life (Level, decreasing, return of premium, annually renewable; Special features: Renewable, Convertible)
Annuities (Single & flexible premium, Immediate & deferred, Fixed & variable, Indexed, Accumulation/Annuity periods, Payout options)
Combination Plans & Variations (Joint life/first-to-die, Survivorship life/second-to-die)

content_outline.domain: II. Life Provisions, Riders, Options, and Exclusions
topics: Policy Riders (Waiver of premium, Guaranteed insurability, Payor benefit, AD&D, Term riders, LTC rider, Cost of Living)
Policy Provisions & Options (Entire contract, Insuring clause, Free look 10 days, Owner's rights, Beneficiary designations)
Premium Payment & Reinstatement (Modes, Grace period 31 days, Automatic premium loan, Reinstatement rules)
Loans, Surrenders & Non-forfeiture (Policy loans, Cash surrender, Reduced paid-up, Extended term, Dividend options)
Incontestability, Assignments & Exclusions (2-year incontestability, Absolute/Collateral assignment, Suicide clause 2 yrs, War/Aviation exclusions)
Settlement Options & Accelerated Benefits (Lump sum, Interest only, Fixed period/amount, Life income, Living benefits)

content_outline.domain: III. Completing the Application, Underwriting, and Delivering the Policy
topics: Completing the Application (Required signatures, Changes/corrections, Incomplete applications, Warranties vs representations, Receipts)
Point-of-Sale Disclosures & Regulations (Replacement rules, HIPAA privacy/HIV consent, USA PATRIOT Act/AML, GLBA Privacy)
Underwriting Factors & Sources (Insurable interest at application, Medical Information Bureau/MIB, Fair Credit Reporting Act, Risk classification)
STOLI/IOLI Rules (Stranger-originated life insurance restrictions)
Delivering the Policy (Effective date of coverage, Statement of good health, Policy explanation at delivery)
Contract Law (Elements: Offer/acceptance, Consideration, Competence, Legal purpose; Attributes: Conditional, Unilateral, Adhesion, Aleatory)

content_outline.domain: IV. Retirement, Tax, and Other Insurance Concepts
topics: Third-Party Ownership & Group Life (Key-person insurance, Executive bonus, Group conversion privilege, COBRA)
Business Life Insurance (Buy-sell agreements, Cross-purchase vs Entity purchase)
Tax Treatment of Life Insurance (Income tax-free death benefit, Cash value withdrawals FIFO, Policy loan taxation, Surrender gain)
Modified Endowment Contracts (MECs, 7-Pay test, LIFO taxation, 10% early penalty prior to 59½)
Section 1035 Exchanges (Tax-free exchange of Life to Life, Life to Annuity, Annuity to Annuity)
Qualified Retirement Plans (IRAs, Roth IRAs, 401(k), Keogh, Rollover rules)

content_outline.domain: I. Types of Policies
topics: Traditional Whole Life Products (Ordinary, Limited-pay, Single-premium)
Interest/Market-Sensitive Products (Universal life, Variable whole life, Variable universal life, Indexed life)
Term Life (Level, decreasing, return of premium, annually renewable; Special features: Renewable, Convertible)
Annuities (Single & flexible premium, Immediate & deferred, Fixed & variable, Indexed, Accumulation/Annuity periods, Payout options)
Combination Plans & Variations (Joint life/first-to-die, Survivorship life/second-to-die)

content_outline.domain: II. Life Provisions, Riders, Options, and Exclusions
topics: Policy Riders (Waiver of premium, Guaranteed insurability, Payor benefit, AD&D, Term riders, LTC rider, Cost of Living)
Policy Provisions & Options (Entire contract, Insuring clause, Free look 10 days, Owner's rights, Beneficiary designations)
Premium Payment & Reinstatement (Modes, Grace period 31 days, Automatic premium loan, Reinstatement rules)
Loans, Surrenders & Non-forfeiture (Policy loans, Cash surrender, Reduced paid-up, Extended term, Dividend options)
Incontestability, Assignments & Exclusions (2-year incontestability, Absolute/Collateral assignment, Suicide clause 2 yrs, War/Aviation exclusions)
Settlement Options & Accelerated Benefits (Lump sum, Interest only, Fixed period/amount, Life income, Living benefits)

content_outline.domain: III. Completing the Application, Underwriting, and Delivering the Policy
topics: Completing the Application (Required signatures, Changes/corrections, Incomplete applications, Warranties vs representations, Receipts)
Point-of-Sale Disclosures & Regulations (Replacement rules, HIPAA privacy/HIV consent, USA PATRIOT Act/AML, GLBA Privacy)
Underwriting Factors & Sources (Insurable interest at application, Medical Information Bureau/MIB, Fair Credit Reporting Act, Risk classification)
STOLI/IOLI Rules (Stranger-originated life insurance restrictions)
Delivering the Policy (Effective date of coverage, Statement of good health, Policy explanation at delivery)
Contract Law (Elements: Offer/acceptance, Consideration, Competence, Legal purpose; Attributes: Conditional, Unilateral, Adhesion, Aleatory)

content_outline.domain: IV. Retirement, Tax, and Other Insurance Concepts
topics: Third-Party Ownership & Group Life (Key-person insurance, Executive bonus, Group conversion privilege, COBRA)
Business Life Insurance (Buy-sell agreements, Cross-purchase vs Entity purchase)
Tax Treatment of Life Insurance (Income tax-free death benefit, Cash value withdrawals FIFO, Policy loan taxation, Surrender gain)
Modified Endowment Contracts (MECs, 7-Pay test, LIFO taxation, 10% early penalty prior to 59½)
Section 1035 Exchanges (Tax-free exchange of Life to Life, Life to Annuity, Annuity to Annuity)
Qualified Retirement Plans (IRAs, Roth IRAs, 401(k), Keogh, Rollover rules)

annuity.license_type_note: Fixed annuities are sold under the Life line of authority — no separate annuity producer license in this state.
annuity.reciprocity_notes: Fixed annuities sold under the Life LoA; no separate annuity license. NAIC Model 275 best-interest 4-hr one-time training adopted. Newly added from 'no requirement' under the prior 2010 model.
annuity.source_quote: All 50 states have adopted the NAIC Model #275 best-interest standard for annuity sales; producers must complete a one-time 4-credit annuity training course approved by the Department of Insurance. WY is among the adopting jurisdictions.

mortgage_protection.license_type_note: Mortgage protection is sold under the Life LoA - no separate license required.
mortgage_protection.marketing_rule_notes: Ad must not use a policy name/title misrepresenting coverage's true nature (mortgage protection is term life); cannot imply affiliation with mortgage lender or government; cannot be untrue, deceptive, or misleading. Must identify carrier and product as life insurance; use approved form numbers; substantiate guaranteed-acceptance, no-exam, premium, and death-benefit claims; retain ad copies.
mortgage_protection.marketing_rule_statute: Wyo. Stat. §§ 26-13-101 to 26-13-124 [UTPA, NAIC Model 880]
